behavior_in_task_description.reason: All file paths, required strings (utf8Slice, latin1Slice, utf8Slice.call(uint8, pos, end), utf8Slice.call(buffer, sourceStartPos, sourceStartPos + sourceByteLen)), and forbidden tokens (TextDecoder, decodeStr, textDecoder) are explicitly listed in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md 'Required Patterns' section dictates exact code: '{ utf8Slice, latin1Slice } = Buffer.prototype' and 'utf8Slice.call(uint8, pos, end)' and 'utf8Slice.call(buffer, sourceStartPos, sourceStartPos + sourceByteLen)', leaving no reasoning about what to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact literal strings like 'utf8Slice.call(uint8, pos, end)' and '{ utf8Slice, latin1Slice } = Buffer.prototype'; alternatives like `buffer.utf8Slice(pos, end)` or different variable orderings would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests also run `cargo check`, `cargo test`, `cargo fmt` subprocesses for pass_to_pass verification, not just text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: F2P tests require specific source content changes that a stub cannot satisfy; need real edits to the named files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requirements (utf8Slice destructuring, utf8Slice.call usage, no TextDecoder/decodeStr, comment updates, Rust compilation) are each covered by corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped under clearly commented sections (fail-to-pass, pass-to-pass, integration) with descriptive function names and docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code modification, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and identifiers in instruction.md appear consistent with tests and solution patch.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly names exact files to modify and exact code patterns including 'utf8Slice.call(uint8, pos, end)', eliminating any localization/design work.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, not an agentmd/config edit task.
